name: Venison Steaks with Chestnuts & Figs
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt half a tablespoon of butter in a heavy saucepan.
2. Add a good grinding of black pepper and gently cook the chopped green onions.
3. Add 200 mL port and reduce the mixture.
4. Add 300 mL stock and 24 peeled chestnuts to the pan.
5. Simmer for about 20 minutes or until the sauce becomes syrupy. Set aside and keep warm.
6. Heat a heavy-based frying pan and add a tablespoon of butter.
7. When very hot, cook 8 venison medallions, each about 1 cm thick.
8. Sear the steaks for a maximum of 1 minute on each side to ensure they are still rare.
9. Meanwhile, butter a baking tray and slice onto it 4 figs.
10. Place the sliced figs under a grill until heated through.
11. Divide the sauce and chestnuts between four heated plates.
12. Add 2 medallions of venison and a fan of fig slices to each plate.
13. Serve immediately.
